commentaries.author: George Leo Haydock
commentaries.content: 1 Esdras Wisdom of Solomon Wisdom of Sirach Epistle of Jeremiah Prayer of Manasseh Let not them that are my enemies wrongfully rejoice over me: neither let them wink with the eye that hate me without a cause. Hebrew, "liars "(St. Jerome; Haydock) alluding to Saul (Calmet) Christ explains this of himself, John xv. (Worthington) Who. Some supply a negation. (Berthier) "Neither let them wink with the eye who. "(Protestants) This sign might indicate friendship, or evil machinations, Proverbs vi. (Calmet) "They pretended by their looks what they did not entertain in their hearts. Augustine) (Menochius).

methodology.step_1: Parse main page with BeautifulSoup for structure
methodology.step_3: Use Firecrawl to get full content from 'Go to Commentary' links
methodology.step_4: Fallback to BeautifulSoup for accordion content if needed
advantages: Records verses even without commentaries for complete tracking
